TOYOTA MOTOR CORPORATION
Body Repair Manual

2023 TOYOTA CAMRY
LE / SE / XLE / XSE

REAR QUARTER PANEL REPLACEMENT PROCEDURE

Section: Body Panel Operations

REMOVAL PROCEDURE

Step 1 — Preparation
Ensure vehicle is on a level surface. Engage parking brake. Disconnect
the negative battery terminal.

Step 2 — Interior Removal
Remove the rear seat cushion, seat back, and quarter trim panel.
Disconnect any wiring harness clips attached to the inner panel.

Step 3 — Rear Combination Lamp Assembly
Remove the three 10mm bolts securing the rear combination lamp.
Disconnect the wiring connector. Remove the lamp assembly.

Step 4 — Rear Bumper Cover
Remove the clips along the lower edge of the bumper cover.
Remove the screws behind the rear wheel arch. Pull the bumper
cover rearward and disconnect any fog lamp connectors.

Step 5 — Quarter Panel Removal
Using a spot weld drill bit (8mm), drill out all factory spot welds
along the flange areas. Remove any structural adhesive using a
utility knife and heat gun at 80°C maximum.

Cut location: rear pillar lower section per panel sectioning
guidelines. Use TOYOTA-approved sectioning templates.

INSTALLATION PROCEDURE

Step 6 — Panel Preparation
Clean all mating surfaces. Apply 3M 08008 weld-through primer to
bare metal flanges. Allow 15 minutes dry time before welding.

Step 7 — Panel Fitting
Position the new quarter panel. Verify all gaps and reveals:
- Door to quarter gap: 4.0mm +/- 1.0mm
- Lid to quarter gap: 3.5mm +/- 1.0mm

Clamp panel at all weld flanges before welding.

Step 8 — Welding
Perform spot welds per original weld location map. Minimum
weld specifications: 6mm nugget diameter, 25mm pitch spacing.
Use GYS MIGS 200 or equivalent approved MIG welder.

CAUTION: Do not heat UHSS panels above 150°C during repair.

Step 9 — Corrosion Protection
Apply cavity wax to all enclosed sections via access holes.
Apply anti-corrosion sealer to all weld joints and panel flanges.
Apply 2K epoxy primer within 4 hours of metal preparation.

See CORROSION PROTECTION section for complete requirements.

Advisory: This procedure is advisory guidance derived from repair documentation.
Always verify against the current TOYOTA Collision Repair Guide for the
specific vehicle and model year.
